title: Data Center Technician
description:
Overview
  
As a Data Center Technician (DCT) within Microsoft's Cloud Operations & Innovation (CO+I) organisation, you will help build, maintain, and support the infrastructure that powers Microsoft's global cloud services.
  
In this hands-on role, you will deploy and configure hardware, perform troubleshooting and diagnostics, and support hardware refreshes and decommissioning activities. You will follow established operational procedures while helping to ensure the reliability, security, and performance of Microsoft's datacentre environment.
  
This role provides an excellent opportunity to grow your technical expertise, deepen your understanding of datacentre operations, develop mentoring skills, and gain exposure to industry-leading standards in security, hardware management, and operational excellence.
  
CO+I is the team behind Microsoft's cloud platform, supporting services such as Azure, Office 365, Bing, Xbox, and OneDrive. Our employees benefit from extensive training opportunities, professional certifications, career development programmes, and a culture that encourages continuous learning and growth.
  
Microsoft operates more than 200 datacentres across 32 countries, supporting over 1 billion customers and 20 million businesses worldwide. Sustainability, innovation, and operational excellence remain at the heart of our global infrastructure strategy.
  
Our mission is to empower every person and organisation on the planet to achieve more. Guided by our values of respect, integrity, and accountability, we foster an inclusive culture where everyone can thrive.
  
Responsibilities
  
Deploy, install, configure, and stage datacentre hardware according to established standards and procedures.
  
Perform diagnostics and troubleshooting to quickly identify and resolve hardware issues while minimising operational impact.
  
Replace faulty components and perform hardware repairs following standard operating procedures.
  
Conduct post-deployment quality checks, ensuring equipment is correctly staged, labelled, grounded, and cabled.
  
Execute hardware decommissioning activities, including upgrades, refreshes, and simple infrastructure changes.
  
Follow established incident management processes, escalating issues to appropriate technical and operational teams when required.
  
Support and provide guidance to technicians working on complex tasks and projects.
  
Complete role-specific training and continuously develop technical knowledge and operational expertise.
  
Collaborate effectively with teammates by sharing knowledge, participating in team discussions, and supporting operational goals.
  
Demonstrate accountability for service quality, operational excellence, and the overall customer experience.
  
Maintain compliance with Microsoft security, safety, and data management requirements.
  
Embody Microsoft's culture and values in all aspects of work.
  
  

Qualifications
  
Required Qualifications
  
High School Diploma, GED, or equivalent.
  
Basic knowledge of computer hardware, components, and IT infrastructure.
  
Experience supporting IT equipment, technology systems, or related technical environments.
  
  

Preferred Qualifications
  
Additional experience supporting IT hardware, datacentre infrastructure, or related technologies.
  
  

**Industry certifications such as:**  

CompTIA A+
  
CompTIA Server+
  
CompTIA Network+
  
Basic Structured Cabling (BSC)
  
  

Security Requirements
  
Candidates must be able to meet Microsoft, customer, and/or government security screening requirements, including:
  
Successful completion of the Microsoft Cloud Background Check upon hire or transfer and every two years thereafter.
  
  

This role offers an opportunity to develop valuable technical skills while contributing to the infrastructure that powers some of the world's most widely used cloud services. Through hands-on experience, training, and mentorship, you will build a strong foundation for career growth within Microsoft's global datacentre operations organisation.

title: Senior AI Engineer
description:
Sana is an AI lab building superintelligence for work. We believe organizations can accomplish their missions faster when teams can effortlessly access knowledge, automate repetitive work, and learn anything with the help of agentic AI. As part of Workday, we are committed to building AI that augments people - not replaces them.
  
We bring this mission to life through two products. Sana Agents provide a seamless way to access all your company’s apps, knowledge, and data, enabling AI agents to do real work so teams can process and act on information at unprecedented scale. Sana Learn is an AI-powered learning hub that combines the simplicity of a modern learning platform with intelligent features like an AI tutor, automated content generation, and interactive apps, making knowledge not just accessible but actionable.
  
We’re a talent-dense, product-obsessed team of engineers and designers from companies like Google, Spotify, Apple, and Databricks, united by deep technical excellence and rapid iteration. Our tools already help over a million people learn and work better across hundreds of leading enterprises - and we’re just getting started.
  
About the Role
  
You'll build the core agent infrastructure that powers Sana's mission to bring superintelligence to work. This is a greenfield opportunity to define how AI agents plan, reason, and execute across enterprise environments—building systems that reliably handle real-world complexity at scale. You'll work at the intersection of agent architecture, context-, tool- and prompt engineering, and production infrastructure.
  
In this role, you will
  
Architect multi-step planning, orchestration, and tool routing for agents
  
Implement code generation agents and sandboxed code execution
  
Engineer memory, state, and context packing/grounding strategies
  
Balance latency, quality, and cost controls for agent execution
  
Develop safe fallbacks, graceful degradation and robust error handling
  
Collaborate with platform and search teams to deliver reusable agent infrastructure
  
Establish safety guarantees and measurable quality improvements
  
About You
  
  

**Basic Qualifications:**  

3+ years of software engineering experience building production backend or platform systems.
  
3+ years of experience in TypeScript, with a strong track record of writing reliable, maintainable services.
  
3+ years of experience with distributed systems, APIs, asynchronous workflows, and service-oriented architecture.
  
3+ years of experience designing systems with a focus on scalability, reliability, observability, and maintainability.
  
  

**Other Qualifications:**  

Experience building and deploying LLM-powered applications in production.
  
Experience building agent platforms or AI infrastructure.
  
Deep understanding of the low-level details of the OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic LLM APIs, including tool calling, system prompt caching, etc.
  
Familiarity with LLM application patterns, including tool calling, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), memory and context management, multi-step orchestration, and human-in-the-loop systems.
  
Experience building and running machine learning systems in production, including compiling training and test datasets, building training pipelines, evaluating models, and detecting and handling drift (neural networks, Gaussian models, Thompson sampling, etc.).
  
Experience designing evaluation frameworks for LLM or agent quality and safety, including hands-on use of platforms such as Langfuse or LangSmith.
  
Familiarity with vector databases, prompt and context engineering, and experimentation tooling.
  
Experience working with sandbox environments such as Modal, and designing strict access control models to keep user data safe and encrypted at all times.
  
Experience running services in Kubernetes-based environments on GCP or equivalent cloud platforms.
  
Comfort working with Postgres and Redis in high-throughput, low-latency service contexts.
  
Contributions to open source TypeScript projects.
  
Ability to navigate ambiguity, make strong technical tradeoffs, and drive projects from concept to production.
  
Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to partner effectively across engineering, product, and AI research teams.
company_description: By unifying HR and Finance on an open, trusted platform powered by AI, Workday delivers high-impact outcomes our customers can count on.

title: AI Solutions Engineer – Manager/Senior Associate – Tax & Legal Technology
description:
Would you like to drive scalable AI Execution Across Tax & Legal?
  
  

About the Role
  
  

KPMG Tax & Legal in Sweden is expanding its Tax & Legal Technology team and is looking for a hands-on AI Solutions Engineer to turn AI ambitions into practical solutions across our Tax & Legal service lines with tax and legal consultants. This role focuses on building and scaling AI use cases to transform day-to-day work, ensuring that our teams can leverage AI effectively and safely in their delivery. You will translate complex business needs into AI-driven tools and workflows that improve efficiency, quality, and compliance in our Tax & Legal practice. Working closely with our AI Lead (who drives AI strategy, governance, and adoption), the AI Solutions Engineer drives execution and delivery - together forming a balanced partnership of control and execution to achieve enterprise-level AI adoption. You will collaborate across service lines and with Nordic colleagues, ensuring solutions align with KPMG’s Global and EMA AI initiatives and standards.
  
  

What you will do
  
  

**Build & Implement AI Solutions:** Design, develop, and deploy AI-powered solutions that address real business problems in Tax & Legal. You will create AI personas, prompts, and agents using firm-approved platforms like Digital Gateway GenAI, Microsoft 365 Copilot (including Copilot Studio), aIQ, and Lovable. This includes rapid prototyping and testing of proof-of-concepts to demonstrate value early. You will integrate large language models (LLMs) and automation workflows to embed AI into daily work, moving solutions from pilot to production.
  
**Enable & Scale Use Cases Across Service Lines:** Engage closely with Tax & Legal teams and AI Champions to identify high-value AI opportunities and translate business needs into standardized AI solutions. You will replicate and scale successful use cases across multiple service lines, ensuring that solutions are reusable and standardized rather than one-off experiments. Partner with service line stakeholders to adapt AI tools to their workflows, ensuring broad adoption and consistent usage.
  
**Ensure Compliant & Practical Adoption:** Operate within governance frameworks and policies to ensure all AI solutions meet security, data privacy, and risk management requirements. You will embed best practices in prompt engineering, persona development, and AI workflow design to optimize performance while upholding responsible AI principles. Work in tandem with the AI Lead and Risk/Legal teams to review solutions that involve sensitive data or client use, ensuring compliance and ethical standards from design to deployment. By aligning with KPMG’s Nordic, EMA, and Global AI directives, you will ensure our local solutions are both innovative and secure.
  
  

Why This Role is important
  
  

AI investments risk remaining theoretical without strong execution. Service lines need practical, hands-on support to embed AI in their day-to-day work. This role is the execution engine that takes AI from ambition to reality - bridging the gap between strategic vision and on-the-ground implementation. By focusing on scalable, compliant solutions, the AI Solutions Engineer ensures that our AI initiatives do not remain siloed experiments but become reliable tools that transform how our professionals deliver value. Scaling requires standardization; without this role’s expertise, AI efforts might stay isolated and underutilized. With you in place, good ideas will become working solutions that are widely adopted across Tax & Legal.
  
  

Work with great people
  
Julia Blomqvist
  
Deal Advisory
  
**"The 3 best things about working at KPMG are:** The people: Ambitious colleagues who become friends. Responsibility: Significant from day one, fostering growth. Development: Constantly improving in a supportive environment."
  
Amit Verma
  
Audit
  
"Working for KPMG has expanded my impact globally. In a diverse and inclusive team, we collaborate across cultures, helping me understand global markets and design broad-reaching solutions. This experience allows me to make a meaningful difference for our clients."
  
Martin Ericsson
  
Deal Advisory
  
"I continually face new challenges requiring a mix of skills. Over 25 years, I've worked with both small startups and billion-dollar enterprises. Regardless of the client's size, my job lets me meet interesting people and help them find unique solutions."
  
Skills & Requirements
  
**Hands-On AI Engineering Expertise:** Strong programming skills and experience building AI or software solutions (3-5+ years in a role such as AI Engineer, or similar). Proven ability to code in languages like Python, work with APIs and automation scripts, and develop end-to-end solutions integrating ML/AI components. Familiarity with modern LLM API platforms such as OpenAI and Anthropic, and frameworks for prompt orchestration or agent development such as Microsoft Copilot Studio are required.
  
**AI & ML Knowledge:** Deep understanding of Generative AI and LLMs, including prompt engineering and fine-tuning concepts. Hands-on experience implementing large language model solutions. Familiarity with machine learning pipelines, and knowledge of data preprocessing, and basic evaluation techniques is beneficial.
  
**Enterprise Technology & Cloud Proficiency:** Experience developing and deploying solutions in an enterprise environment - including familiarity with cloud platforms (especially Azure or comparable cloud services) and architecture for integrating AI solutions securely at scale. Understanding of IT security and data privacy (e.g., identity management, access controls, GDPR considerations) to ensure compliance when handling sensitive data. Experience with enterprise AI platforms and tools, for instance, Microsoft 365 Copilot ecosystem or similar internal platforms is a strong advantage.
  
**Collaboration & Business Mindset:** Excellent ability to work cross-functionally with business domain experts (Tax & Legal professionals) to gather requirements and refine use cases. Strong communication skills to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders, and to document solutions and train users on new AI tools. A consultative approach and understanding of professional services or regulated industries (Tax/Legal/Finance) is a plus, enabling you to better tailor AI solutions to business context.
  
**Innovation & Problem-Solving:** Curiosity and adaptability to the fast-evolving AI landscape. You stay current with new models, frameworks, and best practices in AI/ML, and you are eager to experiment with new tools for practical benefit. You bring a creative mindset to surmount technical challenges and find pragmatic solutions to business problems with AI.
  
**Education & Qualifications:** A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, Information Technology, or a related field. Relevant industry certifications (e.g., Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Engineer, Azure Data Scientist Associate, or OpenAI/Deep Learning AI specializations) are a plus.
  
**Language skills:** Fluency in English is required and a strong proficiency in Swedish is preferred.
  
  

Success in This Role Looks Like
  
  

Within your first year, you will have built several AI solutions that are being actively used by our Tax & Legal teams in Sweden, resulting in clear efficiency gains and quality improvements in service delivery. You will have scaled successful prototypes into standardized tools used across multiple service lines, reducing duplicated effort and increasing reuse of AI capabilities. Business stakeholders will report that AI is making their daily work faster and more consistent, broadening AI adoption across the practice. At the same time, all deployed solutions will be fully compliant and properly governed, with key risks mitigated. You will have accelerated the value realized from our AI investments by turning ideas into tangible results.
  
  

Location & ways of working
  
  

This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden, working in a Nordic collaboration model. Other locations in Sweden may be considered for the right candidate. We offer a hybrid work environment – while you will collaborate closely with business stakeholders in Sweden and across Nordic countries, we support flexible work arrangements. You will be part of a diverse, tech-savvy Tax Transformation unit, with a supportive culture that encourages innovation and knowledge-sharing across borders.
  
  

You will join a dynamic Tax Transformation unit at KPMG that is pioneering new ways of working with AI. You will work in a collaborative, inclusive environment where innovation is encouraged and your impact is visible to senior leadership and across the business. This is a unique opportunity to shape the future of Tax & Legal services with the power of AI, backed by the resources and brand of one of the world’s leading professional services firms.
  
  

We are seeking an enthusiastic AI Solutions Engineer with a forward-thinking mindset, ready to challenge established processes and help our professionals work smarter with AI. If you are excited to build and deploy real-world AI solutions and enable others to embrace AI in their daily work, we encourage you to apply.
  
  

We are looking for candidates based in Sweden who hold a valid work permit.
  
  

What we offer
  
  

At KPMG, you become part of a corporate culture where the individual is important. We offer strong development opportunities in a diverse and dynamic environment where you will be challenged through exciting and socially meaningful collaboration with internal stakeholders and, indirectly, clients.
  
  

You will have a varied and responsible role in a fast-paced environment, with opportunities for:
  
  

Continuous learning and knowledge development
  
Building strong professional networks
  
Contributing to impactful and visible transformation initiatives
  
  

KPMG provides a comprehensive internal training and development program, enabling you to grow within your areas of interest.
  
  

We are looking for professionals who are passionate about driving digital transformation, with a forward-thinking mindset and the ability to challenge established processes and ways of working.
  
  

Due to the summer holiday period, feedback and updates regarding the recruitment process will be provided from mid-August onwards.
  
  

We look forward to receiving your application!
  
  

About the company
  
  

At KPMG, we know that if we use and act on our insights together with the right people, we find opportunities everywhere. With 2000 employees in Sweden, Latvia and Lithuania, and a network of more than 265 000 experts in 143 countries across the globe, we are one of the world’s leading professional services firms.
  
  

We offer expertise within advisory, audit and tax and legal services to many of our region’s leading companies. We may support their organizational change, provide advice regarding risk and compliance, ESG reporting or development journeys, cyber threats, technology transformation, or finance strategy optimization. We also have experts within analysis, M&A and transactions, audit, tax and legal.

title: Digital Solutions Engineer – Automation, Data & Analytics, and Platforms – Senior Manager/Manager – Tax & Legal Technology
description:
Ready to drive scalable automation, data solutions, and configure platforms in Tax & Legal services?
  
  

We are looking for an experienced Digital Solutions Engineer to join our Tax & Legal Technology team in Sweden. In this senior role (Senior Manager or Manager, depending on experience), you will bridge business needs and technology delivery by designing and implementing automation, data & analytics, and platform solutions that transform how our Tax & Legal practice operates. This is a hands-on position focused on building integrated, low-code solutions – moving our operations from fragmented, manual processes to seamlessly automated, data-driven workflows. You will collaborate closely with tax and legal professionals and the Nordic technology team to deliver impactful digital improvements. It is a unique opportunity to play a visible role in our digital transformation, where your work will drive efficiency gains, data insights, and innovative service delivery across Tax & Legal.
  
  

About the role
  
  

As a Digital Solutions Engineer, you will be the technical problem-solver linking Tax & Legal experts with enabling technologies. You will translate business requirements into practical solutions and lead end-to-end development of those solutions – from initial design through configuration, integration, test, and rollout. You will work across a spectrum of low-code platforms and data tools to automate workflows, build analytics capabilities, and configure enterprise software for optimal use. Importantly, you will ensure our local solutions align with global platforms and architecture to maximize reuse and scalability (for example, contributing to our Digital Gateway data ecosystem initiative). In short, you act as a builder and integrator, delivering solutions that enhance both internal operations and client offerings.
  
  

What you will do
  
  

**Automation & Workflow Transformation:** Design and implement end-to-end automation solutions using tools like Microsoft Power Automate (Power Platform) and Alteryx, replacing manual processes with standardized, scalable digital workflows. Identify high-impact automation opportunities in Tax & Legal processes, then build workflows or RPA bots to streamline and optimize these processes. Ensure automation solutions follow best practices for governance and security while delivering measurable efficiency gains.
  
**Data & Analytics Enablement:** Build data pipelines and analytical workflows to enable real-time insights and data-driven decisions. Develop data transformation processes (e.g., using Alteryx or Azure data services) and lightweight analytical models that ensure accurate, timely data is available to consultants and leadership. Work with BI tools (e.g., Power BI) to create dashboards or reports that turn raw data into actionable insights for our Tax & Legal teams.
  
**Platform Configuration & Optimization:** Configure and manage enterprise SaaS platforms (for example, specialized tax software like Kalkit) to meet business needs. This involves bridging standard platform functionality with tailored configurations, so our users get maximum value with minimal custom code. You will implement configuration changes, forms, or workflows within these platforms, ensuring they integrate with our broader systems landscape. In doing so, you act as a link between vendor solutions and internal requirements, making sure platforms are optimally tuned for our Tax & Legal use cases.
  
**Digital Gateway Integration:** Serve as a key contributor to our global Digital Gateway data ecosystem, integrating local automation and analytics solutions into the wider KPMG platform framework. You will collaborate with global technology teams to align local solutions with global standards (avoiding one-off local silos) and to leverage global data platforms for local needs. This ensures we build scalable solutions that can be reused and that local innovations contribute to global initiatives.
  
**End-to-End Solution Delivery:** Integrate automation, data, and third-party systems into cohesive, cross-platform solutions rather than isolated tools. For each initiative, you will design the overall solution architecture, then configure or develop the necessary components (automated workflows, data pipelines, APIs/integrations) to deliver a connected outcome. You will manage the solution lifecycle from prototyping through test, deployment, and user adoption, coordinating with the firm’s centralized IT department and business stakeholders to ensure smooth implementation.
  
  

Throughout these responsibilities, you will work iteratively – gathering requirements together with Business Technology Leads and business stakeholders, rapidly prototyping in low-code environments, and refining solutions based on feedback. You will also contribute to change management and training, helping end-users embrace new tools and ways of working as we transform our operations.
  
  

Work with great people
  
Julia Blomqvist
  
Deal Advisory
  
**"The 3 best things about working at KPMG are:** The people: Ambitious colleagues who become friends. Responsibility: Significant from day one, fostering growth. Development: Constantly improving in a supportive environment."
  
Amit Verma
  
Audit
  
"Working for KPMG has expanded my impact globally. In a diverse and inclusive team, we collaborate across cultures, helping me understand global markets and design broad-reaching solutions. This experience allows me to make a meaningful difference for our clients."
  
Martin Ericsson
  
Deal Advisory
  
"I continually face new challenges requiring a mix of skills. Over 25 years, I've worked with both small startups and billion-dollar enterprises. Regardless of the client's size, my job lets me meet interesting people and help them find unique solutions."
  
Skills & Requirements
  
**Proven experience in digital solution delivery:** You have ~5-7+ years’ experience in roles at the intersection of business and technology (e.g., automation engineer, digital solutions developer, or similar), preferably in consulting or a complex corporate environment. Hands-on track record of designing and delivering digital solutions (automation workflows, data analytics projects, and software implementations) that improved business outcomes. You excel at understanding business processes and translating requirements into tangible solutions.
  
Technical proficiency (low-code, automation & data tools, and configuration experience): Hands-on expertise with a range of automation and analytics technologies. In particular, you have strong experience with Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps for app development, Power Automate for workflows/RPA), Alteryx for data preparation and automation, and Azure cloud services. You are comfortable integrating solutions via APIs and building data pipelines/ETL flows (e.g., using Azure Data Factory or similar). Familiarity with additional RPA tools, analytics platforms (e.g., Databricks, or Qlik), and basic scripting (SQL, Python) is a plus. Most importantly, you are a fast learner able to quickly adopt new cloud-based tools and technologies as the ecosystem evolves.
  
**Strategic problem-solving and design mindset:** You can see the “big picture” and design solutions that are scalable and reusable, rather than point fixes. You approach problems analytically, mapping out processes and data flows to identify root causes and improvement opportunities. You ensure that architecture alignment and governance are built into your solutions (balancing innovation with compliance and risk considerations).
  
**Stakeholder collaboration & communication:** Excellent interpersonal skills with an ability to work closely with non-technical stakeholders (tax and legal experts) and tech teams alike. You can translate technical concepts into business-friendly language and inspire confidence in technology solutions. Experience running workshops, discussing requirements, and providing user training or support is valuable. You are adept at managing multiple priorities across different business areas and can influence and guide decisions to ensure successful adoption of new tools.
  
**Domain knowledge:** Experience in Tax & Legal, finance or compliance-related processes is highly meritorious. While you will receive support to learn domain specifics, a background in professional services or corporate finance environment helps you grasp context and identify relevant use cases faster.
  
**Education & language:** A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., Information Systems, Computer Science, Engineering, or Business with a tech focus). Professional certifications in automation and data (e.g., Alteryx, Power Platform, Azure) are a plus.
  
Fluency in English is required and a strong proficiency in Swedish is preferred.
  
  

Location & ways of working
  
  

This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden, working in a Nordic collaboration model. Other locations in Sweden may be considered for the right candidate. We offer a hybrid work environment – while you will collaborate closely with business stakeholders in Sweden and occasionally across Nordic countries (some travel may be needed for key meetings or workshops), we support flexible arrangements. You will be part of a diverse, tech-savvy Tax Transformation unit, with a supportive culture that encourages innovation and knowledge-sharing across borders.
  
  

We are looking for candidates based in Sweden who hold a valid work permit.
  
  

What we offer
  
  

At KPMG, you become part of a corporate culture where the individual is important. We offer strong development opportunities in a diverse and dynamic environment where you will be challenged through exciting and socially meaningful collaboration with internal stakeholders and, indirectly, clients.
  
  

You will have a varied and responsible role in a fast-paced environment, with opportunities for:
  
  

Continuous learning and knowledge development
  
Building strong professional networks
  
Contributing to impactful and visible transformation initiatives
  
  

KPMG provides a comprehensive internal training and development program, enabling you to grow within your areas of interest.
  
  

We are looking for professionals who are passionate about driving digital transformation, with a forward-thinking mindset and the ability to challenge established processes and ways of working.
  
  

Due to the summer holiday period, feedback and updates regarding the recruitment process will be provided from mid-August onwards.
  
  

We look forward to receiving your application!

At KPMG, we know that if we use and act on our insights together with the right people, we find opportunities everywhere. title: Security Consultant - IAM / PAM
description:
Location: MALMÖ,SWEDEN
Category: Other
7882617
Other
Full-time
Orange Cyberdefense
  
**Contract duration :**

Are you passionate about helping to build a safer digital society? Would you like to join Sweden’s leading cybersecurity company? If so, this could be the opportunity you’ve been looking for!**What you will do**


In your work with digital identities, you help clients strengthen their identity security by combining strategic advisory services, architecture design, and technical implementation to ensure that the right people have access to the right resources in alignment with business objectives and security requirements.


As a Security Consultant with us, you will be part of a team alongside other key cybersecurity specialists. We value your ambition to challenge yourself and grow in your consulting role—through training, exposure to new products, or by exploring new types of projects. Example of job tasks:

* Integrate IAM platforms with HR systems, cloud platforms, and business applications to enable secure and seamless identity flows.
* Contribute to the development and maintenance of policies, frameworks, and procedures for consistent and audit-compliant identity management, as well as manage privileged accounts (PAM).

**We are looking for someone who has experience with:**

* A strong understanding of IAM principles, frameworks, and regulatory requirements.
* Knowledge of identity and authentication standards.
* An understanding of how PAM integrates with IAM, IGA, and Zero Trust frameworks.

**The following qualifications are considered an advantage:**

* Microsoft Active Directory
* Microsoft Entra ID
* CyberArk
* One Identity Manager
* SailPoint
* Okta
* OpenText Identity Manager
* Clavister idAuth
* Thales CipherTrust Secrets Management
* HashiCorp Vault

**Who are you?**

Are you experienced in Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) and looking to join a workplace where knowledge sharing and collaboration are fundamental values? Do you enjoy working as part of a team and driving change with the right priorities and high efficiency? If so, this might be a position for you!

**About the Role**


The role involves project-based work within IT and information security, helping our clients reduce business risks and strengthen their cybersecurity capabilities. We work during regular business hours, Monday through Friday, and value face-to-face interaction with our clients whenever possible. However, remote work from our offices is also a common part of how we operate.


Depending on the assignment, the role may include both leading and governing initiatives as well as actively participating in transformation and improvement projects.


Examples of responsibilities include:

* Conducting cybersecurity maturity assessments for clients.
* Performing risk assessments and risk management activities together with clients.
* Carrying out compliance reviews and gap analyses within regulatory and governance frameworks.

**We Are Looking for Someone Who Has Experience With:**

* Working with, or a strong understanding of, information security management systems such as ISO 27001, the NIS2 Directive, and frameworks such as NIST CSF 2.0 or CIS Controls.
* Data security and information protection.
* Coaching and leading colleagues and/or clients.
* Proven experience in IT security and/or information security
* Professional proficiency in both Swedish and English, spoken and written.

**Meritorious Experience**


The following qualifications are considered an advantage:

* Experience in leadership roles within projects, such as Project Manager or positions involving information security expertise.
* Experience supporting sales activities or working in a customer-facing sales role.
* Relevant cybersecurity certifications, such as CISSP, CISM, or equivalent.
* Knowledge of the Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) and the AI Act.
* Cloud security experience.
* Experience with the Microsoft security product suite, including technologies such as Purview and Data Loss Prevention (DLP).

**Who are you?**


We are looking for someone who shares and embodies our core values: **Simplicity, Humble, Innovation, Respect and Trust**.


You are a strong communicator who can build trust with customers, understand their needs, and translate requirements into practical and effective recommendations. You thrive in a dynamic environment, take initiative, and approach challenges with a solution-oriented mindset, viewing them as opportunities to create value and drive improvement.


Collaboration comes naturally to you, and you enjoy working as part of a team where knowledge sharing, mutual support, and collective success are highly valued. Finally, we are looking for someone who thrives in an innovative industry that is constantly evolving and changing.

**Who are we?**


Orange Cyberdefense is Sweden’s leading cybersecurity company. Our vision is to help secure the digital society. title: Receptionist Scandic Östersund Syd, deltid 65%
description:
Ansök nu
* Ansök senast: 9 augusti 2026
* Östersund
* Arbete på plats
**Arbetsuppgifter**
-------------------

På Scandic Östersund Syd bor våra gäster bara några minuter söder om centrum och det är i Östersund det händer! Oavsett om våra gäster vill spendera dagen i Storsjöbadet eller gå på ett sportevenemang ska de bo hos oss. Äventyrliga barn och badsugna hittar Storsjöbadet och Multi Challenge med Boda Borg precis runt hörnet.



Våra gäster är det viktigaste hos oss och att vi levererar service till dem på den högsta nivån möjligt är vårt främsta fokus. Det gör vi genom en stark servicekultur och ett hotell i toppklass men den allra viktigaste biten är de som gör det otroliga arbetet för vi ska nå dit- våra medarbetare!



**Nu söker vi en Receptionist till Scandic Östersund Syd.**



Oavsett varför gästen kommer till oss är det få saker som är så uppskattade som ett vänligt ansikte som hälsar välkommen. Du agerar värd tillsammans med övriga teamet på hotellet och skapar en gästupplevelse i världsklass.



Hos oss får du ta ett stort eget ansvar för att ta de beslut som krävs för att göra gästen nöjd och teamen utvecklar och förbättrar arbetssätt och service i takt med att omvärlden förändras. Vi är nämligen övertygade om att det är när du får utvecklas och bidra med all din kompetens och erfarenhet som vi överträffar gästens förväntningar. På köpet får vi kul på jobbet!



Så ta med dig dina erfarenheter, ditt engagemang och ditt sätt att tänka till oss – och bidra till en kultur där vi bryr oss på riktigt: om varandra, våra gäster och vår planet. Välkommen till Scandic!


**Vad är innebär rollen?**



Du som arbetar i receptionen är ansvarig för att våra gäster får service i världsklass. Du är spindeln i nätet som ser till att gästen alltid är i fokus. Du är den som går igenom ankomstlistan och lägger tid på detaljerna. Ser till att gästens önskemål uppfylls. Du har alltid ett leende på läpparna och en kreativ lösning i bakfickan. Du löser det mesta och är en stjärna på att vända en missnöjd gäst till en nöjd gäst. Din arbetsplats är vår lobby. För det är där du möter flest gäster. I ansvarsområdet ingår även administrativt arbete, säkerhetsarbete och arbete med försäljning.


Arbetet i receptionen innebär att checka in och ut hotellets gäster, hälsa välkommen och ge gästen den information den behöver om sin vistelse. Du hanterar bokningar i systemet Opera Cloud och hanterar administrativa uppgifter runt gäster och säkerhet. Allt arbete sker med gästnöjdhet och försäljning i fokus.


Hotellet har öppet dygnet runt och det är därför viktigt att du trivs med att arbeta i skift (förmiddagar, eftermiddagar, nätter och helger).


**Vem är du?**



För att passa i rollen tror vi att du har en väl utvecklad social förmåga och är kapabel att hantera att det händer många olika saker samtidigt. Det är mycket viktigt att du kan förmedla ett gott värdskap till hotellets gäster.



Du har också vana och kunskap i att arbeta med dator som arbetsredskap.


Eftersom arbetet till stor del består av att kommunicera med gäster är goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska en förutsättning. Talar du ytterligare ett språk är det meriterande.


Allra viktigast är dock dina personliga egenskaper och din inställning. För att passa i rollen behöver du vara en lyhörd och prestigelös lagspelare som tar ett stort ansvar för dina arbetsuppgifter och helhetsresultatet.


**Information om tjänsten**



Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning på deltid.


Vi tar enbart emot ansökningar via vår hemsida. Alla ansökningar kommer att behandlas löpande och tjänsten kan tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag, varför vi ber dig att ansöka så snart som möjligt. I denna rekrytering använder vi oss av ett enklare test om service vid urvalet.



Vi ser fram emot att välkomna dig till oss på Scandic!



Ps. på vår hemsida kan du läsa mer om vår kultur, vad tjänsten innebär och om våra förmåner!

title: Strategic Partnerships Lead
description:
**Introducing evroc**


At evroc, we are building a secure, sovereign, and sustainable hyperscale cloud to reimagine the digital future of Europe. By joining our company, you have an exciting opportunity to contribute to the development of next-generation cloud services. We are seeking intellectually curious and highly motivated team members who are ready to embrace the thrilling challenge of building the first European owned hyperscale cloud.

**Location:** Stockholm

**Job type:** Permanent position

**Join the Pioneers**


Some careers follow a path. Others change its direction. A once-in-a-generation opportunity to help build the future of enterprise AI in Europe. Every decade produces a new technology platform. We're looking for a Strategic Partnerships Lead to who is exceptionally ambitious, and who wants to help build one of Europe's most important technology companies via their exceptional sales experience.
  

  

Not someday. Now.  

  

This role is designed for someone whose ambition currently exceeds their job title.  

If you're looking for a role where you'll learn more in two years than most people do in ten, keep reading.


Mainframes.  

Client-server.  

The internet.  

Cloud.  

Now AI.  

  

Companies that define these shifts become foundational to how organisations operate.  

  

At evroc, we're building roc; an AI-native operational platform designed to help enterprises manage, govern, orchestrate, and scale AI across their organisation. We believe the next generation of enterprise technology will not simply be about where workloads run. It will be about how AI operates. How it is governed. How it delivers value safely, predictably, and at scale. roc is being built to solve that challenge. Beneath roc sits evroc's sovereign cloud platform; trusted European infrastructure designed for organisations operating in an increasingly AI-driven world. This is not simply another cloud company. This is an opportunity to help build the operational foundation for the next era of enterprise technology.

**What You'll Tackle**


No two weeks will look the same.  

  

* Help shape strategic partnerships with global technology leaders.
* Build relationships with executives, founders, investors, and ecosystem leaders.
* Launch new growth initiatives and strategic programmes.
* Create new opportunities across Europe.
* Work alongside sales, marketing, product, engineering, and leadership teams.
* Support commercial negotiations and strategic planning.
* Identify emerging AI and technology trends.
* Represent evroc at industry events and executive meetings.
* Use AI tools to amplify impact and accelerate execution.

  

This is a highly visible role with meaningful ownership from day one.**Skills We Value**


We don't believe great people come from one specific background. You may have worked in sales, business development, strategy, consulting, venture capital, marketing, startups, operations, product, partnerships, or something entirely different.  

  

You may have two years of experience, you may have eight. What matters is not where you've worked. It's how you think.  

  

We're looking for people with:  

  

* Exceptional ambition.
* High intelligence and curiosity.
* Strong commercial instincts.
* The ability to build trust and relationships.
* A bias for action.
* The confidence to operate in unfamiliar environments.
* A genuine interest in technology, AI, and the future of enterprise innovation.

  

If you've consistently been one of the strongest performers in the room, we'd love to hear from you. No specific technology partnership experience is required. No specific number of years in technology is required. Potential, learning velocity, and execution matter more than job titles.**Why Join evroc?**


AI isn’t a future skill here. It’s part of how we work today. We expect every member of our team to leverage AI to think faster, automate repetitive work, uncover insights, and amplify their impact. If you're already using AI as a force multiplier, you'll fit right in.


You'll join at a stage where every decision matters. You'll work directly with senior leaders. You'll help shape strategic decisions rather than simply execute them. You'll have access to some of Europe's most influential technology companies. You'll build relationships that most people spend a decade trying to reach. You'll spend more time with senior executives, founders, investors, and technology leaders in your first year than many people do in their first decade.  

  

Most importantly, you'll be able to look back in ten years and say:  

  

'I was there when it was being built.’


Here’s what you’ll get:

* Direct exposure to senior executives and strategic decision-making.
* Exceptional learning and career acceleration.
* Significant responsibility from day one.
* Mentorship from experienced technology leaders.
* Competitive compensation.
* Employee stock options.
* The opportunity to help build Europe's next great technology company.
* A front-row seat to one of the most exciting AI stories in Europe.


There are moments in a career when timing matters. This is one of them.  

  

AI is reshaping every industry.  

  

evroc is building the technology that will help enterprises operate in that future.  

  

This is not a job. It’s a launchpad. Come and help build something that matters.

**Why Join evroc?**


We offer a competitive salary and an equity package to attract the best. Whether it’s the cosmopolitan allure of London, the vibrant Stockholm, or the Mediterranean charm of Sophia Antipolis, you’re poised for an inspiring work environment and a captivating local culture!


At evroc, diversity is our strength. We champion an inclusive environment where every background - ethnicity, age, gender identity, beliefs, and culture - is celebrated.


Applicants must possess a valid work permit.

***Please note: Due to summer holidays, the interview process for this role may experience extended response and scheduling times during July and early August.***

title: Data Strategy & Engieering
description:
Role Overview
  
We are seeking a visionary and technically proficient IT Strategy and Data Engineer to bridge the gap between our business objectives and our technical data capabilities. You will design, build, and optimize robust data pipelines and architectures, while concurrently architecting long-term IT and data strategies that drive company growth.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
Data Engineering & Architecture
  
  

**Pipeline Development:** Design, construct, and maintain scalable data pipelines and API integrations for complex, high-volume datasets.
  
**ETL/ELT Workflows:** Build and manage data warehousing solutions using automated Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) frameworks.
  
**Data Governance & Quality:** Implement data quality assurance frameworks and enforce data security and privacy compliance standards.
  
**Database Management:** Optimize database performance (e.g., query optimization, indexing, partitioning) to ensure efficient data availability. [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
  
  

IT & Data Strategy
  
**Strategic Planning:** Collaborate with executive leadership and cross-functional teams to shape the long-term data platform and enterprise architecture roadmap. [1]
  
**Vendor & Tool Evaluation:** Research, evaluate, and recommend open-source technologies, third-party vendor tools, and cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure). [1, 2]
  
**Business Alignment:** Translate high-level business requirements into technical solutions and establish metrics to measure the strategic impact of new data solutions. [1, 2]
  
**Cross-Functional Leadership:** Partner with Data Analysts and Data Scientists to design data models that empower business intelligence (BI)

title: Enterprise Account Executive
description:
##### **About Proxify**


Founded in 2018 and headquartered in Stockholm, Proxify helps 2,500+ companies globally scale their tech teams with top-tier talent in Software, Data and AI. When internal demand for tech skills arises, we deliver carefully vetted, tailored developers within 48 hours removing traditional hiring delays so clients can move fast.


Recognised by the Financial Times as the 3rd fastest-growing company in Sweden (2025), and a member of the Deloitte Fast 50 Sweden (2024), we’re trusted by startups, scale-ups and global brands like Securitas, Electrolux and Lufthansa.

##### **Role overview**


As an Enterprise Account Executive, you will play a key role in Proxify's Enterprise New Business function. This is a strategic opportunity to help shape and scale how we acquire new enterprise clients while driving revenue through outbound prospecting, inbound lead management, and expansion opportunities within existing enterprise groups.


You will own the full sales cycle, from first outreach to signed agreement, while helping refine and scale the processes, playbooks, and ways of working that will support the continued growth of our Enterprise team.

##### **Key responsibilities**

* Own the full sales cycle, from prospecting and discovery through proposal, negotiation, and closing new Enterprise clients.
* Drive outbound prospecting through research, multi-channel outreach, qualification, and meeting generation.
* Manage inbound Enterprise opportunities with speed and commercial focus.
* Identify and develop new business opportunities within subsidiaries, business units, and affiliated companies of existing Enterprise customers.
* Build trusted relationships with senior business and technical stakeholders to understand their hiring and technology needs.
* Collaborate closely with Account Managers to ensure smooth client handovers after successful onboarding.
* Work cross-functionally with Marketing, Client Engineering, and Delivery teams to create an exceptional customer experience.
* Help establish scalable sales processes, playbooks, and best practices that will support the continued growth of the Enterprise New Business function.
* Maintain accurate pipeline management, forecasting, and CRM hygiene while consistently working towards ambitious revenue targets.

##### **Must-have**

* 4+ years of experience in B2B SaaS, technology services, staffing, outsourcing, or recruitment sales, with a strong focus on new business acquisition.
* Proven track record of consistently exceeding quota and closing complex Enterprise B2B deals.
* Experience managing the full sales cycle from outbound prospecting through contract negotiation and closing.
* Comfortable engaging with C-level executives, senior decision-makers, and technical stakeholders.
* Strong commercial mindset with excellent negotiation and relationship-building skills.
* Self-driven, proactive, and comfortable creating structure in a fast-moving environment.
* Ability and willingness to travel 1–2 business trips per month as needed.
* Advanced written and spoken English.
* Experience working with CRM systems and sales forecasting tools.

##### **Nice-to-have**

* Experience selling tech services, developer staffing, IT consulting, or software solutions.
* Experience selling into Enterprise organisations within international markets.
* Understanding of software development, AI, cloud technologies, or modern engineering organisations.
* Experience building or scaling new sales functions, processes, or playbooks.
* Comfortable working in distributed teams and remote-first environments.
* Existing network of enterprise decision-makers.

##### **Why you will love this opportunity**


Help tech teams close skill gaps and create job opportunities for tech talent worldwide.


️ Be part of a humble, supportive, and tech-savvy team.


Drive Enterprise growth by winning new strategic customers globally.


Great opportunities for professional development and personal growth.


️ Work in a truly international team of 39 countries, with occasional in-person meetups.

##### **Why it may not be your match**


You prefer highly structured environments with established processes rather than helping build them.


You enjoy account management more than hunting for and closing new business.


You lack curiosity about technology or feel uncomfortable discussing technical challenges.

##### **How to apply**


Please send your CV (in English) explaining:

* Your experience acquiring and closing new Enterprise clients.
* An example of a complex B2B or Enterprise deal you've successfully won.
* Why Proxify's mission excites you and what motivates you about this opportunity.

title: Service Delivery Manager
description:
**Vill du vara den som får teknik, affär, leverans och kundrelationer att samverka på riktigt?**


På IT-Total söker vi nu en Service Delivery Manager som vill ta ett helhetsansvar för våra kundleveranser och långsiktiga kundrelationer. I rollen kombinerar du strategiskt kundansvar med operativ leveransledning och driver både den dagliga leveransen och införandeprojekt för nya och befintliga kunder.


Du blir en nyckelperson mellan kund, leveransorganisation och affär med ansvar för att säkerställa hög kundnöjdhet, stark leveranskvalitet och kontinuerlig utveckling av våra tjänster. Samtidigt leder du förändrings- och implementationsprojekt där du samordnar resurser, driver aktiviteter framåt och ser till att projekt genomförs enligt plan.


Hos oss får du stort ansvar, stort förtroende och stora möjligheter att påverka. Rollen passar dig som trivs i skärningspunkten mellan teknik, ledarskap och affär och som vill skapa långsiktigt värde genom både framgångsrika leveranser, väl genomförda projekt och ständiga förbättringar.

**Om rollen**


Som Service Delivery Manager på IT-Total ansvarar du för att utveckla och förvalta våra kundrelationer samt säkerställa hög kvalitet i våra leveranser. Du arbetar nära kunder, konsulter, operations, och säljare för att skapa långsiktigt värde och en leveransupplevelse i toppklass.


Rollen befinner sig i gränslandet mellan affär, teknik och leverans där du agerar kundens främsta kontaktperson i leveransfrågor och håller ihop helheten internt. Du säkerställer att våra serviceavtal, servicenivåer och leveransåtaganden uppfylls samtidigt som du identifierar förbättringsmöjligheter och driver utvecklingen av våra tjänster.


Utöver det löpande leveransansvaret kommer du att leda införandeprojekt för nya kunder samt driva förändringsinitiativ hos befintliga kunder. Det innebär att du planerar, samordnar och driver implementationer av nya tjänster, miljöer och lösningar tillsammans med våra tekniska specialister och kundteam. Du ansvarar för att aktiviteter genomförs enligt plan, att rätt resurser engageras och att kunden får en professionell och trygg upplevelse genom hela införandet – från uppstart till etablerad leverans.


Vi ser också att du har ett intresse för hur AI, automation och nya tekniska möjligheter kan användas för att effektivisera processer, utveckla våra tjänster och skapa ännu större värde för våra kunder.

**Dina ansvarsområden**

* Ansvara för kundrelationer, kundnöjdhet och leveranskvalitet.
* Säkerställa att avtalade servicenivåer och leveransåtaganden uppfylls.
* Leda återkommande servicemöten och uppföljningar med kunder.
* Följa upp incidenter, analysera statistik och presentera relevanta insikter.
* Vara kundens huvudsakliga kontaktperson och eskaleringspunkt i leveransfrågor.
* Förvalta befintliga serviceavtal och delta i framtagning och förhandling av nya avtal.
* Leda onboarding och införandeprojekt för nya kunder.
* Planera, koordinera och säkerställa en framgångsrik övergång från försäljning till leverans.
* Driva implementation av nya tjänster, lösningar och miljöer hos både nya och befintliga kunder.
* Koordinera interna resurser och säkerställa framdrift i både leverans och projekt.
* Identifiera möjligheter till förbättring genom automation, AI-stöd och smartare arbetssätt.
* Driva utveckling av tjänster, processer, verktyg, rutiner och dokumentation.

**Vi tror att du**


Är en naturlig ledare som kombinerar ett starkt kundfokus med struktur, affärsmässighet och driv. Du trivs med att ta ansvar, fatta beslut och få saker att hända. Samtidigt har du förmågan att skapa förtroende hos kunder, kollegor och leverantörer genom tydlig kommunikation och ett lösningsorienterat arbetssätt.


Du är självgående, prestigelös och van att prioritera, delegera och hantera flera parallella aktiviteter. Du känner dig bekväm med att arbeta utifrån avtal, hantera krav, risker och avvikelser samt säkerställa både leveranskvalitet och lönsamhet.

**Vi ser gärna att du har**

* Erfarenhet som Service Delivery Manager, Leveransledare, Projektledare eller liknande roll inom IT.
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av att leda införandeprojekt, onboardingprojekt, transitionsprojekt eller större förändringsinitiativ.
* God förståelse för projektmetodik, planering, riskhantering och resurskoordinering.
* Erfarenhet av att hantera serviceavtal, SLA:er och kunddialoger.
* Förmåga att kombinera ett kommersiellt perspektiv med operativ leverans.
* Erfarenhet av att driva förbättringsarbete och effektivisering.
* Erfarenhet av eller intresse för AI, automation och digitalisering som verktyg för att utveckla processer, tjänster och kundleveranser.
* God teknisk förståelse inom IT-infrastruktur, Private Cloud, Public Cloud och närliggande tjänster.
* Flytande svenska och engelska i tal och skrift.

 **Vad som gör att du trivs i rollen hos oss**


Hos IT-Total får du en roll med stort mandat och stor variation. Ena dagen leder du ett strategiskt servicemöte med kundens beslutsfattare, nästa dag driver du ett införandeprojekt eller koordinerar interna team för att säkerställa en framgångsrik leverans.


Du blir en nyckelperson som påverkar både kundernas utveckling och IT-Totals fortsatta tillväxt. Här får du möjlighet att arbeta i en organisation där engagemang, kvalitet och långsiktiga relationer står i fokus – och där dina idéer och initiativ gör skillnad på riktigt..

**Varför kommer du att trivas på IT-Total?**


IT-Total föddes ur idén att vara det bästa företaget för medarbetare inom sin bransch, därför välkomnar vi dig till en arbetsplats som ger dig glädje, stimulans och möjlighet att växa inom det område du brinner för. Vi är en innovativ arbetsgivare som satsar på din framtid och jobbar i teknikens framkant med ett högt säkerhetstänk. Med säkerhetsprövade medarbetare och säkerhetsklassade lokaler- och datahallar garanterar vi trygghet och integritet för våra kunder.  

  

**Vi erbjuder:**


En himmelsk fredagsfrukost med kollegorna


Hybrid arbetsplats


Roligt på jobbet - allt från träning till resor och spelkvällar


Ett välfyllt lager av frukt, nötter, gudomligt kaffe från en Kunglig Hovleverantör om så önskas.


Fredagsfika tillsammans


Ett stenkast till både shopping på Mall of Scandinavia och natursköna Brunnsviken som är perfekt för löpning och lunchpromenader


Goda och trygga anställningsvillkor.


Frihet att styra din egen utveckling och ditt eget arbete, vi tror på "Work life balance"


‍‍ Kollegor i världsklass som bryr sig


Goda parkeringsmöjligheter


️‍️ Generöst friskvårdsbidrag

 **Varmt välkommen med din ansökan i formuläret nedan.**  

  

**Har du några frågor gällande tjänsten?**  

Tveka då inte att höra av dig till ansvarig rekryterare:  

Kajsa Jordansson, Talent Acquisition Manager, IT-Total  

Mail: kajsa.jordansson@it-total.se
  

Tel: 070 -729 19 82

title: Lead Business Analyst - Data Analysis, Agile methodology
description:
Göteborg, Västra Götalands län
  
Job Summary
  
  

VCC Fieldglass ID 1959 -
  
  

Measurement Data Analyst – Automotive Vehicle Testing
  
  

About the Role
  
  

We are looking for a skilled Measurement Data Analyst to support vehicle-level testing and validation activities within our automotive engineering organization. You will play a key role in transforming raw measurement data from vehicle tests (road, track, and simulation environments) into actionable insights that drive product quality, performance, and robustness.
  
  

This role is central to multi-functional testing (MFT) and data-driven validation , enabling cross-functional teams to understand vehicle behaviour under real-world and scenario-based conditions.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
  

Data Analysis & Interpretation
  
  

Analyse measurement data from vehicle tests (e.g., CAN, Ethernet, sensor data, logging systems)
  
Process large datasets from SIL, HIL, VIL, and physical test drives
  
Identify trends, anomalies, and correlations across vehicle functions
  
  

KPI & Performance Evaluation
  
  

Define, calculate, and track vehicle-level KPIs/KPOIs (e.g., energy efficiency, drivability, thermal performance, ADAS behaviour)
  
Support scenario-based performance evaluation (e.g., urban drive cycles, highway, edge cases)
  
Develop automated pipelines for KPI extraction and reporting
  
  

Root Cause & Fault Analysis
  
  

Investigate deviations and failures observed during testing
  
Correlate signals across multiple ECUs and domains (powertrain, chassis, ADAS, infotainment)
  
Support fault injection analysis and robustness validation
  
  

Testing & Validation Support
  
  

Collaborate with test engineers to define measurement strategies and logging configurations
  
Ensure data quality, synchronization, and traceability
  
Support preparation and analysis of multi-functional test campaigns
  
  

Reporting & Visualization
  
  

Create clear, stakeholder-specific reports (engineering, project management, leadership)
  
Develop dashboards and visualizations for test results
  
Present insights and recommendations in a structured and concise manner
  
  

[hr align="center" size="2" width="100%"]
  
  

Required Qualifications
  
  

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Automotive Engineering, Data Science, or similar
  
3+ years of experience in automotive testing, measurement data analysis, or validation
  
  

**Strong experience with:**  

**Data analysis tools:** Python (pandas, numpy), MATLAB, or similar
  
**Measurement/logging tools:** CANoe, CANalyzer, INCA, ETAS, or equivalent
  
  

**Good understanding of:**  

Vehicle architectures and ECU communication (CAN, LIN, Ethernet)
  
Automotive testing frameworks (V-cycle, ASPICE, ISO 26262 awareness)
  
Skill Requirements
  
  

Skills
  
  

Preferred Qualifications
  
  

**Experience with:**  

Scenario-based and multi-functional testing (MFT)
  
EV-specific analysis (battery, energy consumption, thermal behavior)
  
ADAS/AD data analysis and sensor fusion validation
  
  

**Familiarity with:**  

Big data platforms or cloud-based analytics
  
Automation of data pipelines and test analysis workflows
  
Experience working in cross-functional, agile teams
  
  

[hr align="center" size="2" width="100%"]
  
  

Key Competencies
  
  

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  
Ability to handle complex, multi-domain datasets
  
Structured communication tailored to different stakeholders
  
High attention to detail with focus on data quality and traceability
  
Proactive and collaborative mindset
  
Other Requirements

title: Client Director Nordics
description:
This is an incredible opportunity to be part of a company that has been at the forefront of AI and high-performance data storage innovation for over two decades. DataDirect Networks (DDN) is a global market leader renowned for powering many of the world's most demanding AI data centers, in industries ranging from life sciences and healthcare to financial services, autonomous cars, Government, academia, research and manufacturing. **"DDN's A3I solutions are transforming the landscape of AI infrastructure." – IDC** ***“The real differentiator is DDN. I never hesitate to recommend DDN. DDN is the de facto name for AI Storage in high performance environments” - Marc Hamilton, VP, Solutions Architecture & Engineering | NVIDIA***  

DDN is the global leader in AI and multi-cloud data management at scale. Our cutting-edge data intelligence platform is designed to accelerate AI workloads, enabling organizations to extract maximum value from their data. With a proven track record of performance, reliability, and scalability, DDN empowers businesses to tackle the most challenging AI and data-intensive workloads with confidence.  

Our success is driven by our unwavering commitment to innovation, customer-centricity, and a team of passionate professionals who bring their expertise and dedication to every project. This is a chance to make a significant impact at a company that is shaping the future of AI and data management.  

Our commitment to innovation, customer success, and market leadership makes this an exciting and rewarding role for a driven professional looking to make a lasting impact in the world of AI and data storage.  

DDN is seeking a **Client Director – Strategic AI Infrastructure** to drive revenue growth in the **Nordics** within our Artificial Intelligence business unit. This is a **quota-carrying role** responsible for developing strategic enterprise opportunities and expanding DDN’s presence within organizations building large-scale AI and high-performance computing environments.

  

In this role, the Account Executive will operate as a **Strategic AI Client Director**, partnering with enterprise customers, research institutions, and cloud organizations to deliver high-performance data infrastructure solutions that support advanced AI workloads.


The successful candidate will work closely with internal engineering teams, solutions architects, and channel partners to design and deliver complex AI infrastructure solutions supporting **GPU computing, large-scale model training, and data-intensive workloads**.

**Key Responsibilities*** Act as a **Strategic AI Client Director** within the assigned Nordics region, driving adoption of DDN’s AI infrastructure solutions across enterprise and research organizations.
* Develop and execute **strategic territory and account plans** targeting organizations building large-scale AI and high-performance computing environments.
* Identify and qualify new opportunities across industries including **financial services, healthcare, telecommunications, technology, research institutions, and large enterprise organizations**.
* Build trusted relationships with **senior technical leaders, AI infrastructure teams, and executive decision-makers** within target accounts.
* Partner with solutions architects and engineering teams to design and present **AI infrastructure and high-performance data storage solutions**.
* Deliver compelling presentations, product demonstrations, and proposals showcasing the value of DDN’s AI-optimized storage platforms.
* Manage the full enterprise sales cycle including **pipeline development, forecasting, contract negotiation, and deal closure**.
* Develop strong relationships with **channel partners, system integrators, OEM partners, and distributors** to expand market reach.
* Stay current on market trends related to **AI infrastructure, GPU computing, large-scale model training, and high-performance data environments**.
* Represent DDN at industry events, conferences, and customer engagements to generate new opportunities and strengthen market presence.

**Required Qualifications*** Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience.
* 8+ years of experience in **enterprise technology sales**, preferably within storage, compute, networking, or infrastructure platforms.
* Demonstrated experience selling **AI infrastructure, HPC solutions, cloud platforms, or enterprise data center technologies**.
* Proven track record of **meeting or exceeding sales quotas** in enterprise technology environments.
* Experience managing **complex enterprise sales cycles** involving technical buyers and executive stakeholders.
* Ability to travel within the East Coast territory as needed.

**Preferred Experience*** Experience selling **AI infrastructure, GPU clusters, or high-performance computing platforms**.
* Familiarity with the AI ecosystem including technologies from **NVIDIA, Intel, AMD, Cerebras, Graphcore, or Google TPU**.
* Knowledge of enterprise storage technologies including **SAN, NAS, object storage, and parallel file systems**.
* Experience working with **channel partners, system integrators, and distributor ecosystems**.
* Familiarity with technologies such as **Kubernetes, OpenStack, and large-scale AI data center environments**.

  



**Why Join DDN**
At DDN, you will work at the forefront of AI infrastructure innovation, helping organizations solve some of the most demanding data challenges in the world. Our teams collaborate across engineering, sales, and customer success to deliver cutting-edge technology that powers the next generation of AI breakthroughs.

  

DDN has a very strong orientation towards these 4 characteristics and any successful employee will demonstrate these capabilities: **Self-Starter -** Takes independent action to identify and solve problems. Seeks out relevant information needed to make decisions. Gets involved with new initiatives.**Success/Achievement Orientation -** Delivers quality results consistently. Targets, achieves (or exceeds) measurable results. Sets challenging goals, focuses on critical priorities, and is accountable.**Problem Solving -** Recognizes problems and responds with a systematic assessment that identifies and addresses cause of issue. Practical, realistic, and resourceful.**Innovative -** Builds and improves key business processes that enhance the effectiveness of DDN. Generates new ideas, challenges the status quo, and solves problems creatively. **DataDirect Networks, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer.** All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, gender expression, transgender, sex stereotyping, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, protected Veteran Status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.
